title: LRRK2 kinase inhibition for Parkinson's — the on-target lung gate
query: Is LRRK2 kinase inhibition a viable Parkinson's-disease strategy given the on-target lung safety signal?
headline: LRRK2 genetics support kinase inhibition — but a well-cited on-target AT2 lung lysosomal liability is the program gate.
_provenance_note: discover dossier facts captured LIVE from EMET (Drug Safety workflow, Thorough) on 2026-06-21; real PMIDs. Genetics row is an honest KNOWN_UNKNOWN pending a Target Validation capture. Panel/synthesis are reasoned Bucket-2 judgment citing the dossier.

discover.source: EMET Drug Safety workflow (live 2026-06-21) + internal moat (mock)
discover.summary: A live EMET Drug Safety run on LRRK2 surfaced a well-cited on-target lung liability — AT2 pneumocyte lamellar-body vacuolation from LRRK2 kinase inhibition, recapitulating the Lrrk2-KO phenotype (on-target), with a human iPSC-AT2 translational model, a lysosomal/surfactant mechanism, and a translatable urinary di-22:6-BMP biomarker; rodents show a species gap. Genetics (G2019S gain-of-kinase) is pending a Target Validation capture.
discover.result: LRRK2 inhibition: genetics strong (G2019S; capture pending). The gate is the on-target AT2 lung lysosomal liability and its therapeutic window vs CNS target engagement.

runs.out: Brain-penetrant ATP-competitive LRRK2 inhibitor co-folding is applicable; not run in this capture.

validate.result: Structure/affinity compute is applicable to the LRRK2 kinase–inhibitor pair; not executed in this capture.

panel.headline: Genetics-validated target; the lung liability is on-target and human-translatable
panel.rationale: LRRK2 is among the best human-genetically validated PD targets and kinase inhibition is mechanistically aligned with G2019S gain-of-kinase. But the EMET-captured lung signal is on-target — NHP AT2 lamellar-body vacuolation recapitulates the Lrrk2-KO phenotype (PMID:25653221) and a human primary AT2 model reproduces it dose-dependently (PMID:33049313). That is not a compound problem you medicinal-chemistry your way out of; it is the biology of LRRK2 in lysosomes (PMID:38428793).
panel.top_risk: On-target AT2 lung lysosomal liability shared by any LRRK2 kinase inhibitor
panel.ask: Define the therapeutic window between CNS target engagement and the lung effect in a human-relevant AT2 assay before committing a lead

panel.headline: Approvable only with a translatable lung-safety biomarker and reversibility
panel.rationale: The species gap (PMID:25653221 — rodents spared at relevant doses) means rodent tox will under-predict; NHP and human in vitro are the relevant models, which complicates the safety package. The mitigating factor is a translatable pharmacodynamic readout: urinary di-22:6-BMP falls with inhibition in NHP and KO (PMID:25653221; PMID:40770658), giving a monitorable clinical biomarker. Reversibility on a drug holiday is unproven and would be a review focus.
panel.top_risk: A clinical lung-safety signal with no demonstrated reversibility
panel.ask: Reversibility study + a prospectively-qualified di-22:6-BMP monitoring plan before chronic dosing

panel.headline: Validated target, crowded field, single well-characterized gate
panel.rationale: LRRK2 is a genetically clean PD target with active clinical-stage programs, so the biology risk is low and the differentiation race is real. The lung liability is well-characterized rather than unknown, which is dealable — value hinges on whether a window or a peripheral-sparing/CNS-restricted strategy exists. A milestone-heavy structure gated on the lung de-risking package is the rational deal shape.
panel.top_risk: On-target lung liability caps the therapeutic window and the valuation
panel.ask: Option/milestone deal gated on a human AT2 window + reversibility readout

panel.headline: On-target, human-translatable, mechanism-explained — assume it translates
panel.rationale: Every escape hatch is closed: the effect is on-target (KO recapitulation), reproduced in a human AT2 model, and mechanistically explained via lysosomal surfactant handling. The base case should be that this translates to humans and constrains chronic CNS dosing. Absent a demonstrated window or a CNS-restricted modality, the program is gated before efficacy is testable.
panel.top_risk: There may be no therapeutic window between CNS engagement and lung lysosomal disruption
panel.ask: Show a real human-relevant window, or a CNS-restricted/peripheral-sparing approach, before any program commitment

facts.value: LRRK2 (leucine-rich repeat kinase 2) — brain-penetrant small-molecule kinase inhibitor in scope for Parkinson's.

LRRK2 G2019S gain-of-kinase-function is the most common monogenic cause of PD — supports kinase inhibition. (Capture pending: the Target Validation portion scrolled off the live run; not PMID-cited here yet.)
LRRK2 kinase inhibition causes AT2 pneumocyte lamellar-body vacuolation in NHPs (GNE-7915/GNE-0877); recapitulates the Lrrk2-KO lung phenotype → on-target, not compound-specific.
A human primary AT2 air-liquid-interface model reproduces dose-dependent lamellar-body enlargement → the lung signal likely translates to humans.
LRRK2 regulates AT2 lysosomal function and surfactant-phospholipid processing — the mechanism of the lung liability.
Urinary di-22:6-BMP decreases with LRRK2 inhibition in NHP and Lrrk2-KO → translatable pharmacodynamic / safety biomarker.
Rodents do not show the lung phenotype at pharmacologically relevant doses; NHP + human in vitro are the only predictive models.
Reversibility of the AT2 lung effect on a drug holiday, and the therapeutic window vs CNS target engagement, are unproven.

rebuttal.shift: Unmoved — the lung effect is on-target biology; sharpens the ask to a quantified human AT2 window before lead commitment.
The di-22:6-BMP biomarker (PMID:40770658) makes a monitorable path plausible; holds 3, conditional on a reversibility study.
Confirms a milestone-heavy option gated on the lung-window package; the crowded field rewards a peripheral-sparing differentiator.
Three lenses converge on the same on-target lung gate — reinforces that the window must be proven, not assumed.

synthesize.recommendation: Advance LRRK2 kinase inhibition only behind a lung-safety de-risking package; structure any external deal milestone-heavy on the lung gate.
synthesize.consensus: Genetics-validated PD target (G2019S gain-of-kinase); kinase inhibition is mechanistically sound.
synthesize.dissent: Conviction 3 (CSO, regulator, BD) vs 2 (red-team, who weights the on-target lung liability as a likely hard ceiling). No champion; all conditional/hold.
synthesize.convergent_gate: All four flag the SAME risk: the on-target AT2 lung lysosomal liability and whether any therapeutic window exists vs CNS target engagement.
synthesize.proposed_experiment: A human iPSC-AT2 lamellar-body dose-response + reversibility assay, paired with urinary di-22:6-BMP, to define the therapeutic window between CNS target engagement and the lung effect before FIH. (Also capture the LRRK2 genetics via the EMET Target Validation workflow to close the B1 known-unknown.)
synthesize.confidence: Biology: HIGH. Overall program: CONDITIONAL on the lung gate.
